runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "PHP", "build_system": "docker", "package_manager": "composer", "build_commands": ["composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress"], "install_commands": ["apt-get update", "apt-get install -y git unzip", "rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*"], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "8.2", "system_packages": ["git", "unzip"]}, "repo_type": "library", "base_image": "php:8.2.30-cli-trixie", "base_image_rationale": "Official PHP CLI image pinned to PHP 8.2 to satisfy the project's PHP constraints and provide a consistent build environment.", "pitfalls": ["Only a single PHP version is defined in this blueprint; CI workflows expect multiple PHP versions and OS variants, which may require a matrix build or multi-stage approach to cover all targets.", "The minimal PHP image may not have all extensions enabled by default; explicit installation or enabling of needed extensions (e.g., intl, gd) may be required for tests or usage.", "Composer install will fetch dev dependencies; ensure network access and necessary permissions for pulling packages from external repositories."], "notes": "This blueprint targets packaging a PHP library (PragmaRX Google2FA). A production image could be optimized with a multi-stage build to exclude development dependencies."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1

FROM composer:2 AS builder
WORKDIR /app
# Copy composer metadata first; composer.lock may not exist in this repo
COPY composer.json ./
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress

# Copy the rest of the application code
COPY . .

FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie AS final
# Install essential system tools
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git unzip ca-certificates && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app /app
ENV PHP_MEMORY_LIMIT=512M
# Health endpoint used by smoke tests
RUN /bin/sh -lc 'printf "%s\n" "<?php" "echo \"PHP_OK\";" "?>" > /app/health.php'
CMD ["php", "/app/health.php"]

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie

LABEL maintainer="Automation"

# Install system packages and PHP extensions
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        git \
        unzip \
        curl \
        libicu-dev \
        libxml2-dev \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libzip-dev \
        libpng-dev \
        libjpeg62-turbo-dev \
        libfreetype6-dev && \
    docker-php-ext-configure gd --with-freetype --with-jpeg && \
    docker-php-ext-install -j"$(nproc)" intl xml mbstring gd exif curl zip json pdo pdo_sqlite

# Clean apt lists
RUN rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Composer
RUN curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php -- --install-dir=/usr/local/bin --filename=composer

# Set workdir
WORKDIR /app

# Copy composer files and install dependencies
COPY composer.json composer.json
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress

# Copy source code
COPY . .

# Default command: provide a friendly message when container starts
CMD ["bash","-lc","echo \"Container ready. Run vendor/bin/phpunit or use composer\""]
"}

VerifyBuild status: build_failed

Concerns
- Missing composer.lock handling; without a lock file, builds are non-deterministic. Copy composer.lock and run composer install with it; then copy the rest of the code.
- Copying only composer.json before app code may skip rebuilding vendor on code changes; pattern is to copy both composer.json and composer.lock, install, then copy the rest.
- No .dockerignore to exclude vendor or other large paths; can bloat build context or risk overwriting committed vendor.

Build error
- Exit code: 1
- configure: error: Package requirements (oniguruma) were not met:
  Package 'oniguruma', required by 'virtual:world', not found
  Consider adjusting the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable or setting ONIG_CFLAGS and ONIG_LIBS to avoid pkg-config.

  - Context: Failure occurs during the docker-php-ext-install step after attempting to install system packages; the configure phase reports the missing Oniguruma dependency.

Missing packages or files mentioned
- Oniguruma (Oniguruma library for regular expressions)
- Not in the apt-get install list in RUN; fix is to install libonig-dev (or oniguruma-dev)

Version mismatch info
- Not explicitly shown as a version mismatch. Build uses Debian trixie and PHP 8.2.30-cli-trixie (from the log). The failure is due to a missing Oniguruma package, not a version conflict.

Quick fix suggestion
- Add Oniguruma development package to the apt-get install list, e.g.:
  apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends libonig-dev
- Alternatively ensure PKG_CONFIG_PATH or ONIG_CFLAGS/ONIG_LIBS point to an existing Oniguruma installation.

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie

LABEL maintainer="Automation"

# Install system packages and PHP extensions
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        git \
        unzip \
        curl \
        libicu-dev \
        libxml2-dev \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libzip-dev \
        libpng-dev \
        libjpeg62-turbo-dev \
        libfreetype6-dev \
        libonig-dev && \
    docker-php-ext-configure gd --with-freetype --with-jpeg && \
    docker-php-ext-install -j"$(nproc)" intl xml mbstring gd exif curl zip json pdo pdo_sqlite

# Clean apt lists
RUN rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Composer
RUN curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php -- --install-dir=/usr/local/bin --filename=composer

# Set workdir
WORKDIR /app

# Copy composer files and install dependencies
COPY composer.json composer.json
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress

# Copy source code
COPY . .

# Default command: provide a friendly message when container starts
CMD ["bash","-lc","echo \"Container ready. Run vendor/bin/phpunit or use composer\""]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie

LABEL maintainer="Automation"

# Install system packages and PHP extensions
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        git \
        unzip \
        curl \
        libicu-dev \
        libxml2-dev \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libzip-dev \
        libpng-dev \
        libjpeg62-turbo-dev \
        libfreetype6-dev \
        libonig-dev \
        libcurl4-openssl-dev && \
    docker-php-ext-configure gd --with-freetype --with-jpeg && \
    docker-php-ext-install -j"$(nproc)" intl xml mbstring gd exif curl zip json pdo pdo_sqlite

# Clean apt lists
RUN rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Composer
RUN curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php -- --install-dir=/usr/local/bin --filename=composer

# Set workdir
WORKDIR /app

# Copy composer files and install dependencies
COPY composer.json composer.json
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress

# Copy source code
COPY . .

# Default command: provide a friendly message when container starts
CMD ["bash","-lc","echo \"Container ready. Run vendor/bin/phpunit or use composer\""]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie

LABEL maintainer="Automation"

# Basic system utilities
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        git \
        unzip && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Composer
RUN curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php -- --install-dir=/usr/local/bin --filename=composer

# Set working directory for the build
WORKDIR /app

# Install PHP dependencies (will fetch dev dependencies as needed)
COPY composer.json composer.json
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress

# Copy the rest of the source code
COPY . .

# Default command: provide a friendly message; tests can override this if needed
CMD ["bash","-lc","echo \"Container ready. You can run tests with vendor/bin/phpunit or composer exec commands.\""]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage build to create a PHP library image with dependencies installed
FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie AS builder

LABEL maintainer="Automation"

# Install system dependencies required for PHP extensions
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        git \
        unzip \
        curl \
        libicu-dev \
        libxml2-dev \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libzip-dev \
        libpng-dev \
        libjpeg62-turbo-dev \
        libfreetype6-dev \
        libonig-dev \
        libcurl4-openssl-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Enable and install common PHP extensions used by the project
RUN docker-php-ext-configure gd --with-freetype --with-jpeg && \
    docker-php-ext-install -j"$(nproc)" intl xml mbstring gd exif curl zip json pdo pdo_sqlite

# Set build working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install Composer in the builder stage
RUN curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php -- --install-dir=/usr/local/bin --filename=composer

# Copy composer.json and install dependencies in builder stage
COPY composer.json composer.json
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress

# Copy the rest of the application
COPY . .

FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie

LABEL maintainer="Automation"

# Copy installed dependencies and code from builder stage
COPY --from=builder /app /app

WORKDIR /app

# Default command
CMD ["bash","-lc","echo \"Container ready. Run vendor/bin/phpunit or use composer\""]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie

LABEL maintainer="Automation"

# Basic system utilities
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        git \
        unzip \
        curl && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Composer
RUN curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php -- --install-dir=/usr/local/bin --filename=composer

# Set working directory for the build
WORKDIR /app

# Copy composer.json and install dependencies first (lockfile respected if present)
COPY composer.json composer.json
COPY composer.lock composer.lock 2>/dev/null || true
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress

# Copy the rest of the application source
COPY . .

# Default command: friendly message
CMD ["bash","-lc","echo \"Container ready. You can run tests with vendor/bin/phpunit or composer.\""]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie

LABEL maintainer="Automation"

# Basic system utilities
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        git \
        unzip \
        curl && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Composer
RUN curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php -- --install-dir=/usr/local/bin --filename=composer

# Set working directory for the build
WORKDIR /app

# Copy composer.json and install dependencies first (lockfile respected if present)
COPY composer.json composer.json
# Optional: copy composer.lock if present to ensure deterministic installs; if not present, composer will install without lock
COPY composer.lock composer.lock 2>/dev/null || true
RUN test -f composer.lock && cp composer.lock composer.lock || true
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress

# Copy the rest of the application source
COPY . .

# Default command: friendly message
CMD ["bash","-lc","echo \"Container ready. You can run tests with vendor/bin/phpunit or composer.\""]
"}

steps.tool_input: {"path": "composer.json"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage build to create a PHP library image with dependencies installed
FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie AS builder

LABEL maintainer="Automation"

# Install system dependencies needed for building and composer
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        git \
        unzip \
        ca-certificates && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Composer in the builder stage
RUN curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php -- --install-dir=/usr/local/bin --filename=composer

# Set workdir for the build
WORKDIR /app

# Copy only composer.json to install dependencies first
COPY composer.json composer.json
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress

# Copy the rest of the application
COPY . .

FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie AS final

LABEL maintainer="Automation"

# Copy built application from builder
COPY --from=builder /app /app

WORKDIR /app

# Default command
CMD ["bash","-lc","echo \"Container ready. You can run vendor/bin/phpunit or composer.\""]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie

LABEL maintainer="Automation"

# Install system dependencies and PHP extensions
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        git \
        unzip \
        curl \
        libicu-dev \
        libxml2-dev \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libzip-dev \
        libpng-dev \
        libjpeg62-turbo-dev \
        libfreetype6-dev \
        libonig-dev \
        libcurl4-openssl-dev && \
    docker-php-ext-configure gd --with-freetype --with-jpeg && \
    docker-php-ext-install -j"$(nproc)" intl xml mbstring gd exif curl zip json pdo pdo_sqlite && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set working directory for the build
WORKDIR /app

# Copy composer.json and install dependencies (lockfile will be used if present in the context)
COPY composer.json composer.json
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress

# Copy the rest of the application
COPY . .

# Default command: friendly message
CMD ["bash","-lc","echo Container ready. You can run vendor/bin/phpunit or composer."]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie

LABEL maintainer="Automation"

# Install system dependencies for building PHP extensions
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        git \
        unzip \
        curl \
        libicu-dev \
        libxml2-dev \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libzip-dev \
        libpng-dev \
        libjpeg62-turbo-dev \
        libfreetype6-dev \
        libonig-dev \
        libcurl4-openssl-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Enable and install common PHP extensions used by the project
RUN docker-php-ext-configure gd --with-freetype --with-jpeg && \
    docker-php-ext-install -j"$(nproc)" intl xml mbstring gd exif curl zip json pdo pdo_sqlite

# Install Composer
RUN curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php -- --install-dir=/usr/local/bin --filename=composer

# Set workdir
WORKDIR /app

# Copy composer.json and install dependencies
COPY composer.json composer.json
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress

# Copy the application source
COPY . .

# Default command: friendly message
CMD ["bash","-lc","echo Container ready. You can run vendor/bin/phpunit or composer"]
"}

iterations.injected_lessons:
1. **Issue: Missing `composer.lock` handling → nondeterministic builds**  
   - Cause: Only `composer.json` was copied before `composer install`; `composer.lock` was missing.  
   - Fix: Copy both files explicitly:  
     ```dockerfile
     COPY composer.json composer.lock ./
     RUN composer install --no-dev --no-interaction --prefer-dist
     ```  
     Ensure both files exist; don’t use shell redirection (`2>/dev/null || true`) with `COPY`.

2. **Issue: Invalid Dockerfile syntax in COPY command**  
   - Cause: Commands like `COPY composer.lock composer.lock 2>/dev/null || true` were used to conditionally copy files.  
   - Fix: Dockerfile syntax doesn’t allow conditionals. Always specify exact source/target; check lock existence before build if necessary (`if [ -f composer.lock ]` outside Docker).

3. **Issue: Composer not installed in runtime**  
   - Cause: `RUN composer install` failed because Composer wasn’t included in `php:8.2.30-cli-trixie`.  
   - Fix: Either:  
     - Use a multi‑stage build with `composer` official image for build stage (`FROM composer:2 AS builder`), or  
     - Install Composer manually:  
       ```dockerfile
       RUN curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php -- --install-dir=/usr/local/bin --filename=composer
       ```

4. **Issue: COPY order overwriting vendor directory**  
   - Cause: `COPY . .` after `composer install` overwrote `vendor/` content.  
   - Fix:  
     - Use `.dockerignore` to exclude `vendor/`, `node_modules/`, `.git/`, etc.  
     - Copy only needed code after install, or copy vendor from builder stage:  
       ```dockerfile
       COPY --from=builder /app/vendor ./vendor
       ```

5. **Issue: Using `bash -lc` as CMD caused runtime failure**  
   - Cause: Some PHP CLI images lack `bash`.  
   - Fix: Use `/bin/sh -c` or simply specify an entrypoint known to exist:  
     ```dockerfile
     CMD ["php", "-a"]
     ```  
     or ensure `bash` is installed via `apt-get install -y bash`.

6. **Issue: Missing system packages for PHP extensions**  
   - Cause: Errors involving oniguruma or missing build libraries (`libonig-dev`, etc.).  
   - Fix: Add required dev packages before `docker-php-ext-install`:  
     ```dockerfile
     RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
         libonig-dev libzip-dev unzip git curl && \
         docker-php-ext-install mbstring zip && \
         apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
     ```

7. **Lesson on build order and caching**  
   - Copy and install dependencies (composer) before copying full source for better cache reuse.  
   - Use multi‑stage to reduce final image size and isolate tools.  

8. **Overall improvements for next attempt**  
   - Valid syntax only (no shell logic in Dockerfile directives).  
   - Always include both `composer.json` and `composer.lock`.  
   - Use multi‑stage Composer builder or include Composer explicitly.  
   - Validate runtime CMD with base image capabilities.

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1

FROM composer:2 AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY composer.json composer.lock ./
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress
COPY . .

FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie AS final
# Install essential system tools
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git unzip && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app /app
ENV PHP_MEMORY_LIMIT=512M
CMD ["php", "-r", "echo 'Google2FA library container';"]
"}

steps.tool_input: {"path": "src/Support/QRCode.php"}
steps.tool_output_raw:
<?php

namespace PragmaRX\Google2FA\Support;

trait QRCode
{
    /**
     * Creates a QR code url.
     *
     * @param string $company
     * @param string $holder
     * @param string $secret
     *
     * @return string
     */
    public function getQRCodeUrl(
        $company,
        $holder,
        #[\SensitiveParameter]
        $secret
    ) {
        return 'otpauth://totp/'.
            rawurlencode($company).
            ':'.
            rawurlencode($holder).
            '?secret='.
            $secret.
            '&issuer='.
            rawurlencode($company).
            '&algorithm='.
            rawurlencode(strtoupper($this->getAlgorithm())).
            '&digits='.
            rawurlencode(strtoupper((string) $this->getOneTimePasswordLength())).
            '&period='.
            rawurlencode(strtoupper((string) $this->getKeyRegeneration())).
            '';
    }
}

            rawurlencode(strtoupper($this->getAlgorithm())).
            '&digits='.
            rawurlencode(strtoupper((string) $this->getOneTimePasswordLength())).
            '&period='.
            rawurlencode(strtoupper((string) $this->getKeyRegeneration())).
            '';
    }
}

<?php

/*
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------
| Register The Composer Auto Loader
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------
|
| Composer provides a convenient, automatically generated class loader
| for our application. We just need to utilize it! We'll require it
| into the script here so that we do not have to worry about the
| loading of any our classes "manually". Feels great to relax.
|
*/

require __DIR__.'/../vendor/autoload.php';

/*
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------
| Set The Default Timezone
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------
|
| Here we will set the default timezone for PHP. PHP is notoriously mean
| if the timezone is not explicitly set. This will be used by each of
| the PHP date and date-time functions throughout the application.
|
*/

date_default_timezone_set('UTC');

steps.tool_output_raw:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

function current_directory() {
    SOURCE="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"

    while [ -h "$SOURCE" ]; do # resolve $SOURCE until the file is no longer a symlink
        DIR="$( cd -P "$( dirname "$SOURCE" )" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd )"
        SOURCE="$(readlink "$SOURCE")"
        [[ $SOURCE != /* ]] && SOURCE="$DIR/$SOURCE" # if $SOURCE was a relative symlink, we need to resolve it relative to the path where the symlink file was located
    done

    CURRENT_DIRECTORY="$( cd -P "$( dirname "$SOURCE" )" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd )"
    TESTS_DIRECTORY="$( cd -P "$( dirname "$CURRENT_DIRECTORY" )" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd )"
    ROOT_DIRECTORY="$( cd -P "$( dirname "$TESTS_DIRECTORY" )" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd )"
}
steps.tool_output:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

function current_directory() {
    SOURCE="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"

    while [ -h "$SOURCE" ]; do # resolve $SOURCE until the file is no longer a symlink
        DIR="$( cd -P "$( dirname "$SOURCE" )" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd )"
        SOURCE="$(readlink "$SOURCE")"
        [[ $SOURCE != /* ]] && SOURCE="$DIR/$SOURCE" # if $SOURCE was a relative symlink, we need to resolve it relative to the path where the symlink file was located
    done

    CURRENT_DIRECTORY="$( cd -P "$( dirname "$SOURCE" )" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd )"
    TESTS_DIRECTORY="$( cd -P "$( dirname "$CURRENT_DIRECTORY" )" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd )"
    ROOT_DIRECTORY="$( cd -P "$( dirname "$TESTS_DIRECTORY" )" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd )"
}

# Copy composer metadata first for caching; composer.lock is optional in this setup
COPY composer.json ./
RUN composer install --no-interaction --prefer-dist --no-progress --no-dev --optimize-autoloader

# Copy the rest of the application sources
COPY . .

FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie AS final
# Install essential system tools
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git unzip ca-certificates && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app /app
ENV PHP_MEMORY_LIMIT=512M
# Basic smoke test on startup to ensure PHP CLI works; this is a library container
CMD ["php", "-r", "echo PHP_OK;"]
"}

FROM php:8.2.30-cli-trixie AS final

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git unzip ca-certificates && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

ENV PHP_MEMORY_LIMIT=512M
